facts: The most valuable thing to borrow from claw-code is its interaction contract: live compact activity during a turn, clean final transcript after the turn, and full structured truth in secondary inspectable surfaces.

BioAPEX already has additive session blocks for text, tool use, tool result, retrieval, workflow event, and usage, so the next UX phase can be presentation-first instead of waiting on a deep runtime rewrite.
A good BioAPEX modern-agent UX should separate three layers: turn surface, answer surface, and truth surface.
Compliance should remain severity-based in main chat: quiet for low-risk allow states, prominent for warnings, approval-required states, and blocked states.
BioAPEX should add domain-specific quick actions and context discoverability rather than copying claw-code's full developer-oriented slash-command breadth.
